## Osteoporosis Cohort — Three-Source FHIR Query Set

---

### Query 1 — Documented Diagnosis (Condition / SNOMED CT)

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|64859006
```

> SNOMED CT `64859006` = *Osteoporosis (disorder)* — the canonical top-level concept covering primary, secondary, and postmenopausal subtypes via IS-A hierarchy.

---

### Query 2 — Bisphosphonate Prescriptions (MedicationRequest / RxNorm)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|70934,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|114970
```

| Agent | RxNorm | Route |
|---|---|---|
| Risedronate | `70934` | Oral |
| Zoledronic acid | `114970` | IV |

> Comma-separation applies OR logic within a single parameter, returning patients prescribed **either** agent in one pass.

---

### Query 3 — Radiologic Bone Loss (Observation / LOINC + value filter)

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|38265-5,http://loinc.org|24701-5&value-quantity=le-2.5
```

| LOINC | Description |
|---|---|
| `38265-5` | Bone density \[T-score\] by DXA — general/axial |
| `24701-5` | Bone density \[T-score\] Femoral neck by DXA |

> `value-quantity=le-2.5` captures all T-scores **≤ −2.5**, the WHO threshold for the osteoporotic range. Both landmark measurement sites are covered via OR logic on the code parameter.

---

### Union Logic

```
patients =
  Condition[Query 1].subject
  ∪ MedicationRequest[Query 2].subject
  ∪ Observation[Query 3].subject
```

Each query addresses a distinct evidence pathway (clinical coding → pharmacologic treatment → imaging biomarker); patients may appear in one, two, or all three result sets. Union across subject references produces the complete cohort.
